payload.title: Hosted Scope: offline-first cloud relay (Path A)
payload.description:
Turn Scope into a hosted realtime auth + todo-streaming service WITHOUT abandoning local-first.

## The decision (Path A)
The cloud node is "just another replica" plus a sync transport. Clients keep their local event logs; the server accepts event uploads, stores the canonical log union, runs the EXISTING replay + SCP-110 display-number de-collision server-side unchanged, and fans changes out over SSE/WS. Offline-first survives.

We explicitly rejected Path B (server-as-single-writer SaaS) because it forces us to discard the merge/de-collision logic that is our actual moat and puts us in head-to-head commodity competition with Convex/Supabase/Firebase/Linear. Path A is a strict superset of B from the user's view: a hosted relay serves purely-online users fine, but a central-authority SaaS can never serve the offline user.

Unverified bet worth testing cheaply before heavy build: that "deterministic offline merge for todos" is something people will PAY for, not just a nice engineering story. See the validation spike.

## What ports as-is
- Append-only event log as source of truth ([[106]] event-sourced storage)
- Deterministic replay into disposable SQLite cache
- ULID identity + LWW scalars + grow-only sets + display-number de-collision (SCP-110)
- The realtime client contract: SSE /events, iOS EventStream, web EventSource

## What must change (both paths would need these — table stakes)
- Real multi-tenant identity (the current loopback-bypass + LAN-CA trust model is single-domain by construction and CANNOT stretch) — biggest new surface
- Multi-tenant storage (per-workspace SQLite -> Postgres, possibly + object store for the log)
- External pub/sub for SSE fan-out across >1 app server (in-process EventEmitter dies the moment we scale horizontally)
- Public TLS / real domains / CORS
- Rate limiting, tenant quotas, abuse model, billing

## Phased path
1. Single-tenant cloud hub: deploy existing Express hub, public TLS, swap bearer-token for real login. Proves realtime over WAN.
2. Multi-tenant storage + external pub/sub.
3. Sync-cursor protocol for offline-first clients (where de-collision cashes out).
4. Billing, quotas, teams.

The spike stories under this epic are deliberately open questions. Each is meant for a future agent (or human) to investigate, comment with findings/clarifications, and SPAWN child stories/epics once scope is understood. Do not treat the spikes as the implementation plan — they are the question list that produces the plan.
